name.es: Pan Darnitsky de centeno y trigo

description.en: A roughly even rye-and-wheat loaf named for Kyiv's Darnytsia district, built on an overnight rye levain and a little molasses. Quietly the most common daily bread across Ukraine and Russia for decades.
description.de: Ein annähernd gleichmäßiges Roggen-Weizen-Brot, benannt nach dem Kiewer Stadtteil Darnytsia, aufgebaut auf einem über Nacht geführten Roggen-Levain und einem Hauch Melasse. Unauffällig das häufigste Alltagsbrot in der Ukraine und Russland seit Jahrzehnten.
description.es: Un pan de centeno y trigo a partes casi iguales, llamado así por el distrito de Darnytsia en Kiev, construido sobre un levain de centeno de toda la noche y un toque de melaza. Sin pretensiones, ha sido el pan diario más común en Ucrania y Rusia durante décadas.
description.fr: Un pain à parts presque égales de seigle et de froment, nommé d'après le quartier de Darnytsia à Kiev, bâti sur un levain de seigle nocturne et une pointe de mélasse. Sans prétention, c'est le pain quotidien le plus courant en Ukraine et en Russie depuis des décennies.

origin.region: Named for the Darnytsia district of Kyiv
origin.note: Became a standard GOST-style loaf across the whole USSR, including Russia, where it remains a daily staple.

ingredients.note: split between the levain and final dough

keeping.note: A daily loaf across the former USSR; keeps well wrapped for the better part of a week.

summary.en: Mix rye starter, rye flour and water; ferment 8-10 hours at a warm 30°C until doubled.
Combine the levain with bread flour, more rye flour, water, molasses, sugar and salt.
Mix by hand then with a dough hook a few minutes to a smooth, cohesive dough.
Cover and rest in a warm spot about 1.5 hours.
Shape into pan loaves, dust with rye flour, and proof until just under the rim, about 1.5 hours.
Bake hot with steam for the first stretch, then reduce heat and finish without steam.
Spritz with water and cool completely before slicing.
